commit.message:
fix: ignore submodule dirty state in release clean-tree check

The _bmad-output directory is now managed as a git submodule.
git status --porcelain without flags reports submodule working tree
changes as dirty, causing the release target to fail.

Add --ignore-submodules=dirty so only changes in the main repository
are checked, while local submodule content modifications are ignored.

commit.message:
fix: UX fixes batch — memory editing, model settings precision, loading screen (#183, #184) (#186)

* fix(#183): use hardware tier for default context window (not raw 12GB threshold)

Replace the duplicated 12 GB totalRam check with hardwareProfileDetector.profile.recommendedMaxTokens.
ActivityManager.MemoryInfo.totalMem reports just under 12 GiB on physical devices
(kernel-reserved RAM excluded), so the old threshold silently fell through to 4096 on
12 GB devices like the S23 Ultra. The HardwareProfileDetector already applies a 10 GB
FLAGSHIP threshold and maps FLAGSHIP→8192, MID_RANGE→2048, LOW_POWER→1024.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#183): loading screen text colour on dark background

LoadingContent's Box uses a background modifier which does not update
LocalContentColor. The animated step Text and CircularProgressIndicator
inherited the parent content colour (black) and were invisible on the
dark background.

- Wrap Column in CompositionLocalProvider(LocalContentColor provides
  MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onBackground) so all child composables that
  inherit content colour render correctly.
- Add explicit color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onBackground to the
  animated step Text as belt-and-suspenders.
- Add explicit color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary to
  CircularProgressIndicator.
- Step counter Text already had onSurfaceVariant — left untouched.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#183): model settings sliders with editable number fields

Each SliderRow now renders a compact OutlinedTextField (76 dp) to the
right of the slider so users can type a precise value instead of
dragging.

- isInteger flag controls formatting (no decimals for context window;
  2 dp for temperature / top-p) and keyboard type (Number vs Decimal).
- Dragging the slider updates the text field in real time.
- Committing (keyboard Done) parses the text, clamps to the valid range,
  updates the slider, and calls onValueChangeFinished — same save path
  as the existing slider onValueChangeFinished.
- Invalid / empty text reverts to the current slider value on Done.
- focusManager.clearFocus() on Done dismisses the soft keyboard cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#184): delete button in core memory edit sheet

The CoreMemoryDetailSheet only showed Cancel + Save. A Delete button
with destructive (error) colouring has been added on the left side of
the button row so the layout reads:

  [Delete]  ·  spacer  ·  [Cancel]  [Save]

Clicking Delete first shows an AlertDialog to confirm, then calls
viewModel.requestDeleteCoreMemory(memory.id) followed by
viewModel.closeCoreMemoryDetail() so the sheet dismisses immediately
while Room processes the deletion asynchronously.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#184): episodic memories are now editable with re-embed on save

Previously the episodic detail sheet was read-only with a single Close
button. It now matches the core memory sheet in all respects.

EpisodicMemoryDao
  - Add updateContent(id, content) @Query to UPDATE episodic_memories.

MemoryRepository (interface)
  - Add updateEpisodicMemory(id, newContent, newVector).

MemoryRepositoryImpl
  - Implement updateEpisodicMemory: upsert vec first (atomic-ish), then
    update content. If the vec upsert throws, content write is skipped,
    matching the core memory no-partial-save contract.

MemoryViewModel
  - Add saveEpisodicMemoryEdit(id, content): embeds on Dispatchers.Default,
    calls updateEpisodicMemory, closes the sheet on success, logs on error.
    isSubmitting flag disables Save while the embedding is in-flight.

MemoryScreen
  - Replace read-only Text(memory.content) with OutlinedTextField.
  - Replace Close-only button row with [Delete] · spacer · [Cancel] [Save],
    matching the core memory sheet layout.
  - Delete triggers an AlertDialog then calls requestDeleteEpisodicMemory
    + closeEpisodicMemoryDetail, same pattern as core memory Fix 4.

fix: use docker inspect for canary service-state detection instead of brittle text parsing (#753)

* Initial plan

* fix: add robust service-state detection guidance to canary prompt

Replace brittle docker compose ps text parsing with machine-readable
docker inspect approach in mode-validate-generate.txt canary section.

The canary was failing with 'not ok 1 - app service is not running' even
when the service was healthy, because the AI-generated script used text
matching on docker compose ps output which varies across Compose versions.

Add CRITICAL section instructing the AI to:
1. Resolve container ID with `docker compose ps -q <service>`
2. Inspect state with `docker inspect -f '{{.State.Running}}'` and
   `docker inspect -f '{{.State.Status}}'`
3. Accept both Running=true and Status=running as valid running states
4. Never gate the TAP line on curl/endpoint success alone

commit.message:
fix: boolean false overrides dropped in multi-document helmfiles (#2527) (#2532)

* fix: environment values pollution causing boolean false overrides to be dropped (#2527)

PR #2367 introduced envCopy.Values = values in NewEnvironmentTemplateData,
where values = GetMergedValues() = Defaults + Values + CLIOverrides. This
caused .Environment.Values to include Defaults, so when multi-part helmfiles
re-assigned environment values via {{ toYaml .Environment.Values }}, Defaults
values (e.g. helmDefaults.atomic: true) were written into the environment
Values field. Later, GetMergedValues() applied Values over Defaults, causing
the stale atomic: true to win over the correct atomic: false override.

Fix: set .Environment.Values to Values + CLIOverrides only (excluding Defaults),
so re-assignment patterns don't pollute the Values layer with Defaults.

Signed-off-by: yxx <yxx@users.noreply.github.com>
